privileged_info_summary: The task uses real human motor imagery EEG data from the PhysioNet EEGMMI dataset (Subject S001, Run 04), not synthetic data. The recording filename is OpenBCI-EEG-S001-MotorImagery.txt and screenshots are saved to /home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots. The data contains 8 EEG channels extracted from a 64-channel 10-10 system montage and resampled to 250 Hz with 120-second duration. Channels 3 and 4 (active) correspond to C3 and C4, the primary motor cortex electrodes used in motor imagery brain-computer interfaces, while channels 1,2,5,6,7,8 (inactive) are frontal (Fp1,Fp2), parietal (P7,P8), and occipital (O1,O2) electrodes. The original source data comes from the EEGMMI database at physionet.org.
